Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
add Experiments feature

- Experiment model: tracks name, hypothesis, linked protocol, start date,
  stabilization period, status, outcome rating/notes, and decision
- Experiments page: active experiments with progress bar and weeks remaining,
  ready-to-assess highlight, multi-experiment warning banner, completed and
  abandoned sections
- Start experiment form with live assessment date preview \(JS\)
- Assessment form: outcome rating slider, notes, and continue/pause/stop
  decision \(auto-updates linked protocol status on pause/stop\)
- Experiment offer page: prompted after adding a new active preventative —
  "Would you like to track this as an experiment?"
- Soft warning modal on new/edit protocol when an experiment is active and
  user is activating a protocol — "Add it anyway" or "Wait"
- Dashboard: ready-to-assess banner with direct link when assessment date passed
- Nav: Experiments link added between Protocols and Symptoms \(reordered\)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(cd ~/migraine-tracker && git add templates/base.html templates/checkin.html static/css/style.css && git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
standardize nav icons to outline SVGs; fix checkin loading bug

- All nav icons replaced with 16×16 outline SVGs using stroke="currentColor"
  and stroke-width="1.5": chat bubble, house, 4-pointed star, clipboard,
  conical flask, heart, gear/sunburst — all inherit the inactive grey and
  active purple from existing .nav-link CSS, no colored emoji
- .nav-icon updated to display:flex / align-items:center so SVGs sit
  correctly in the flex row instead of relying on text-align
- Fix checkin loading state: disabled <textarea> fields are excluded from
  form submission by browsers, so the server received an empty message and
  redirected without processing, leaving the typing indicator stuck forever.
  Fix by disabling the button only and setting opacity on the textarea, plus
  adding a proper submitting flag to block Enter-key double-submits

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(source .venv/bin/activate && python -c "
from app import app, db
from database import Experiment
with app.app_context\(\):
    exp = Experiment.query.filter_by\(status='active'\).first\(\)
    if exp:
        print\(f'Testing assess for experiment: {exp.name} \(id={exp.id}\)'\)
        with app.test_client\(\) as c:
            resp = c.get\(f'/experiments/{exp.id}/assess'\)
            data = resp.data.decode\(\)
            print\(f'Status: {resp.status_code}'\)
            checks = [
                \('assess-layout', 'assess-layout' in data\),
                \('assess-data-panel', 'assess-data-panel' in data\),
                \('Episodes / Week', 'Episodes / Week' in data\),
                \('Avg Symptom Scores', 'Avg Symptom Scores' in data\),
                \('assessTrendChart', 'assessTrendChart' in data\),
                \('form still present', 'Complete Assessment' in data\),
            ]
            for name, ok in checks:
                print\(f'  {name}: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)
    else:
        # Try a completed experiment or any
        exp = Experiment.query.first\(\)
        if exp:
            print\(f'No active experiment; found: {exp.name} \(status={exp.status}\)'\)
        else:
            print\('No experiments in DB - assessment page test skipped'\)
" 2>&1)

Bash(source .venv/bin/activate && python -c "
from app import app, db
from database import InviteCode, User
import secrets

# Clean up
with app.app_context\(\):
    User.query.filter\(User.email.like\('debug%'\)\).delete\(\)
    db.session.commit\(\)

with app.test_client\(\) as c:
    with app.app_context\(\):
        code = secrets.token_urlsafe\(12\)
        db.session.add\(InviteCode\(code=code\)\)
        db.session.commit\(\)

    # Register \(follow redirect so session cookie propagates\)
    c.post\('/register', data={
        'email': 'debug@example.com', 'password': 'Test1234!',
        'confirm_password': 'Test1234!', 'invite_code': code,
    }, follow_redirects=True\)

    # Logout \(follow redirect\)
    c.get\('/logout', follow_redirects=True\)

    with c.session_transaction\(\) as sess:
        print\(f'Session after logout: {dict\(sess\)}'\)

    # Try reuse
    resp = c.post\('/register', data={
        'email': 'debug2@example.com', 'password': 'Test1234!',
        'confirm_password': 'Test1234!', 'invite_code': code,
    }, follow_redirects=True\)
    print\(f'Reuse: status={resp.status_code}, has error={b\\"already been used\\" in resp.data}'\)
" 2>&1)

from app import app, db
from database import InviteCode, User
import secrets

# Clean up all test users
with app.app_context\(\):
    User.query.filter\(User.email.like\('debug%'\)\).delete\(\)
    User.query.filter\(User.email.like\('test%'\)\).delete\(\)
    db.session.commit\(\)

with app.test_client\(\) as c:
    with app.app_context\(\):
        code = secrets.token_urlsafe\(12\)
        db.session.add\(InviteCode\(code=code\)\)
        db.session.commit\(\)

    # Register
    c.post\('/register', data={
        'email': 'debug@example.com', 'password': 'Test1234!',
        'confirm_password': 'Test1234!', 'invite_code': code,
    }, follow_redirects=True\)

    # Try a fresh client to avoid cookie issues
    
with app.test_client\(\) as c2:
    # Try to register with reused code \(fresh client, not logged in\)
    resp = c2.post\('/register', data={
        'email': 'debug2@example.com', 'password': 'Test1234!',
        'confirm_password': 'Test1234!', 'invite_code': code,
    }, follow_redirects=True\)
    print\(f'Fresh client reuse: status={resp.status_code}, has error={b\\"already been used\\" in resp.data}'\)
    
    # Also try bad invite code
    resp = c2.post\('/register', data={
        'email': 'debug3@example.com', 'password': 'Test1234!',
        'confirm_password': 'Test1234!',
        'invite_code': 'totally-fake',
    }, follow_redirects=True\)
    print\(f'Bad code: has error={b\\"Invalid invite code\\" in resp.data}'\)

Bash(source .venv/bin/activate && python -c "
from app import app, db, bcrypt
from database import User, InviteCode
import secrets

# Clean up test users from previous runs
with app.app_context\(\):
    User.query.filter\(User.email.notin_\(['admin@baseline.app']\)\).delete\(\)
    InviteCode.query.delete\(\)
    db.session.commit\(\)

print\('── Auth Flow Tests ──'\)
print\(\)

# Test 1-12: Login/logout flow with main client
with app.test_client\(\) as c:
    resp = c.get\('/', follow_redirects=False\)
    ok = resp.status_code == 302 and '/login' in resp.headers['Location']
    print\(f' 1. Unauthed → /login: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    resp = c.get\('/login'\)
    print\(f' 2. Login page loads: {\\"PASS\\" if resp.status_code == 200 else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    resp = c.get\('/register'\)
    print\(f' 3. Register page loads: {\\"PASS\\" if resp.status_code == 200 else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    with app.app_context\(\):
        user = User.query.filter_by\(email='admin@baseline.app'\).first\(\)
        ok = user and user.password_hash and user.email == 'admin@baseline.app'
        print\(f' 4. Existing user migrated: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    resp = c.post\('/login', data={'email': 'admin@baseline.app', 'password': 'Baseline2026!'}, follow_redirects=False\)
    ok = resp.status_code == 302 and '/login' not in resp.headers.get\('Location', '/login'\)
    print\(f' 5. Login success redirects: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    resp = c.get\('/'\)
    print\(f' 6. Dashboard after login: {\\"PASS\\" if resp.status_code == 200 else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    resp = c.get\('/settings'\)
    ok = resp.status_code == 200 and b'Log out' in resp.data and b'admin@baseline.app' in resp.data
    print\(f' 7. Settings has account/logout: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    resp = c.get\('/'\)
    ok = b'nav-link-logout' in resp.data
    print\(f' 8. Sidebar has logout link: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    resp = c.get\('/logout', follow_redirects=False\)
    ok = resp.status_code == 302 and '/login' in resp.headers['Location']
    print\(f' 9. Logout redirects to /login: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    resp = c.post\('/login', data={'email': 'admin@baseline.app', 'password': 'wrong'}, follow_redirects=True\)
    ok = b'Invalid email or password' in resp.data
    print\(f'10. Bad password generic error: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    resp = c.post\('/login', data={'email': 'nobody@baseline.app', 'password': 'anything'}, follow_redirects=True\)
    ok = b'Invalid email or password' in resp.data
    print\(f'11. Bad email generic error: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

# Test 12: Dev routes require auth
with app.test_client\(\) as c:
    resp = c.get\('/dev/reset', follow_redirects=False\)
    ok = resp.status_code == 302 and '/login' in resp.headers['Location']
    print\(f'12. Dev routes require auth: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

# Test 13-16: Registration
with app.app_context\(\):
    code1 = secrets.token_urlsafe\(12\)
    db.session.add\(InviteCode\(code=code1\)\)
    db.session.commit\(\)

with app.test_client\(\) as c:
    resp = c.post\('/register', data={
        'email': 'newuser@example.com', 'password': 'Test1234!',
        'confirm_password': 'Test1234!', 'invite_code': code1,
    }, follow_redirects=False\)
    ok = resp.status_code == 302 and '/onboarding' in resp.headers['Location']
    print\(f'13. Register → onboarding: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    resp = c.get\('/onboarding/step1'\)
    print\(f'14. Logged in after register: {\\"PASS\\" if resp.status_code == 200 else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    with app.app_context\(\):
        inv = InviteCode.query.filter_by\(code=code1\).first\(\)
        ok = inv.used_at is not None
        print\(f'15. Invite marked used: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

# Test 16-19: Validation errors \(fresh client\)
with app.test_client\(\) as c:
    resp = c.post\('/register', data={
        'email': 'new2@example.com', 'password': 'Test1234!',
        'confirm_password': 'Test1234!', 'invite_code': code1,
    }, follow_redirects=True\)
    ok = b'already been used' in resp.data
    print\(f'16. Reused invite rejected: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    resp = c.post\('/register', data={
        'email': 'newuser@example.com', 'password': 'Test1234!',
        'confirm_password': 'Test1234!', 'invite_code': 'fake',
    }, follow_redirects=True\)
    ok = b'already exists' in resp.data
    print\(f'17. Duplicate email rejected: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    resp = c.post\('/register', data={
        'email': 'x@y.com', 'password': 'Test1234!',
        'confirm_password': 'Wrong!', 'invite_code': 'fake',
    }, follow_redirects=True\)
    ok = b'do not match' in resp.data
    print\(f'18. Password mismatch: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    resp = c.post\('/register', data={
        'email': 'x@y.com', 'password': 'short',
        'confirm_password': 'short', 'invite_code': 'fake',
    }, follow_redirects=True\)
    ok = b'at least 8' in resp.data
    print\(f'19. Short password: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

# Test 20: Remember me
with app.test_client\(\) as c:
    resp = c.post\('/login', data={
        'email': 'admin@baseline.app', 'password': 'Baseline2026!', 'remember': 'on',
    }, follow_redirects=False\)
    ok = resp.status_code == 302
    with c.session_transaction\(\) as sess:
        is_perm = sess.get\('_permanent', False\)
    print\(f'20. Remember me \(permanent session\): {\\"PASS\\" if ok and is_perm else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

# Test 21: Create invite \(debug\)
with app.test_client\(\) as c:
    c.post\('/login', data={'email': 'admin@baseline.app', 'password': 'Baseline2026!'}\)
    resp = c.get\('/dev/create-invite'\)
    ok = resp.status_code == 200 and b'Invite Code Created' in resp.data
    print\(f'21. /dev/create-invite works: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

print\(\)
print\('Done!'\)
" 2>&1)

from app import app, db, bcrypt
from database import User

print\('── Account Management Tests ──'\)
print\(\)

# Test change email
with app.test_client\(\) as c:
    c.post\('/login', data={'email': 'admin@baseline.app', 'password': 'Baseline2026!'}, follow_redirects=True\)

    # 1. Wrong password rejected
    resp = c.post\('/settings/email', data={
        'new_email': 'new@baseline.app', 'current_password_email': 'wrong',
    }, follow_redirects=True\)
    ok = b'Current password is incorrect' in resp.data
    print\(f' 1. Change email — wrong password: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    # 2. Invalid email rejected
    resp = c.post\('/settings/email', data={
        'new_email': 'not-an-email', 'current_password_email': 'Baseline2026!',
    }, follow_redirects=True\)
    ok = b'valid email' in resp.data
    print\(f' 2. Change email — invalid email: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    # 3. Successful email change
    resp = c.post\('/settings/email', data={
        'new_email': 'updated@baseline.app', 'current_password_email': 'Baseline2026!',
    }, follow_redirects=True\)
    ok = b'Email updated' in resp.data and b'updated@baseline.app' in resp.data
    print\(f' 3. Change email — success: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    # 4. Change it back
    c.post\('/settings/email', data={
        'new_email': 'admin@baseline.app', 'current_password_email': 'Baseline2026!',
    }, follow_redirects=True\)

# Test change password
with app.test_client\(\) as c:
    c.post\('/login', data={'email': 'admin@baseline.app', 'password': 'Baseline2026!'}, follow_redirects=True\)

    # 5. Wrong current password
    resp = c.post\('/settings/password', data={
        'current_password': 'wrong', 'new_password': 'NewPass123!', 'confirm_new_password': 'NewPass123!',
    }, follow_redirects=True\)
    ok = b'Current password is incorrect' in resp.data
    print\(f' 4. Change password — wrong current: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    # 6. Short new password
    resp = c.post\('/settings/password', data={
        'current_password': 'Baseline2026!', 'new_password': 'short', 'confirm_new_password': 'short',
    }, follow_redirects=True\)
    ok = b'at least 8' in resp.data
    print\(f' 5. Change password — too short: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    # 7. Mismatch
    resp = c.post\('/settings/password', data={
        'current_password': 'Baseline2026!', 'new_password': 'NewPass123!', 'confirm_new_password': 'Different!',
    }, follow_redirects=True\)
    ok = b'do not match' in resp.data
    print\(f' 6. Change password — mismatch: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    # 8. Successful password change
    resp = c.post\('/settings/password', data={
        'current_password': 'Baseline2026!', 'new_password': 'NewPass123!', 'confirm_new_password': 'NewPass123!',
    }, follow_redirects=True\)
    ok = b'Password updated' in resp.data
    print\(f' 7. Change password — success: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    # 9. Still logged in after password change
    resp = c.get\('/settings'\)
    ok = resp.status_code == 200 and b'admin@baseline.app' in resp.data
    print\(f' 8. Still logged in after pw change: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

# 10. Can log in with new password
with app.test_client\(\) as c:
    resp = c.post\('/login', data={'email': 'admin@baseline.app', 'password': 'NewPass123!'}, follow_redirects=True\)
    ok = b'Dashboard' in resp.data
    print\(f' 9. Login with new password: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

    # 11. Old password no longer works
with app.test_client\(\) as c:
    resp = c.post\('/login', data={'email': 'admin@baseline.app', 'password': 'Baseline2026!'}, follow_redirects=True\)
    ok = b'Invalid email or password' in resp.data
    print\(f'10. Old password rejected: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

# Reset password back for future tests
with app.test_client\(\) as c:
    c.post\('/login', data={'email': 'admin@baseline.app', 'password': 'NewPass123!'}, follow_redirects=True\)
    c.post\('/settings/password', data={
        'current_password': 'NewPass123!', 'new_password': 'Baseline2026!', 'confirm_new_password': 'Baseline2026!',
    }, follow_redirects=True\)

# Duplicate email test
with app.app_context\(\):
    if not User.query.filter_by\(email='other@baseline.app'\).first\(\):
        u = User\(name='Other', email='other@baseline.app',
                 password_hash=bcrypt.generate_password_hash\('Test1234!'\).decode\('utf-8'\),
                 onboarding_complete=True\)
        db.session.add\(u\)
        db.session.commit\(\)

with app.test_client\(\) as c:
    c.post\('/login', data={'email': 'admin@baseline.app', 'password': 'Baseline2026!'}, follow_redirects=True\)
    resp = c.post\('/settings/email', data={
        'new_email': 'other@baseline.app', 'current_password_email': 'Baseline2026!',
    }, follow_redirects=True\)
    ok = b'already in use' in resp.data
    print\(f'11. Duplicate email rejected: {\\"PASS\\" if ok else \\"FAIL\\"}'\)

# Clean up
with app.app_context\(\):
    User.query.filter_by\(email='other@baseline.app'\).delete\(\)
    db.session.commit\(\)

print\(\)
print\('Done!'\)
" 2>&1)

load_dotenv\(override=True\)

Bash(git add app.py && git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
fix: replace SQLite PRAGMA commands with SQLAlchemy inspect for migrations

PRAGMA table_info and PRAGMA foreign_keys are SQLite-only and crash on
PostgreSQL. Use sqlalchemy.inspect\(\) to check column existence and
nullability. The peak_severity nullable migration now uses ALTER COLUMN
on PostgreSQL and keeps the table-recreation approach for SQLite.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)" && git push)

from app import app
with app.test_client\(\) as c:
    # Check register page renders
    r = c.get\('/register'\)
    assert r.status_code == 200, f'register: {r.status_code}'
    assert b'pw-strength' in r.data, 'missing strength indicator'
    assert b'pw-match-error' in r.data, 'missing match error'
    assert b'disabled' in r.data, 'submit should be disabled'
    print\('register.html OK'\)
    
    # Check login redirects \(no user session\)
    r = c.get\('/'\)
    assert r.status_code == 302, f'index redirect: {r.status_code}'
    print\('auth redirect OK'\)

Bash(source .venv/bin/activate && python -c "
from app import app, db, User
from flask_bcrypt import Bcrypt

with app.test_client\(\) as c:
    with c.session_transaction\(\) as sess:
        # Create a test session - we need a real user
        with app.app_context\(\):
            user = User.query.first\(\)
            if user:
                sess['user_id'] = user.id
    
    if user:
        # Test pages that need auth
        for path, name in [
            \('/symptoms/new', 'new_symptom'\),
            \('/protocols/new', 'new_protocol'\),
            \('/rescue-options/new', 'new_rescue'\),
            \('/episodes/new', 'new_episode'\),
        ]:
            r = c.get\(path\)
            if r.status_code == 302:
                print\(f'{name}: redirected \(onboarding?\)'\)
            else:
                assert r.status_code == 200, f'{name}: {r.status_code}'
                assert b'maxlength' in r.data or b'max=' in r.data or path == '/episodes/new', f'{name}: missing validation attrs'
                print\(f'{name} OK'\)
        
        # Dashboard
        r = c.get\('/'\)
        if r.status_code == 200:
            print\('dashboard OK'\)
        else:
            print\(f'dashboard: {r.status_code}'\)
    else:
        print\('No user in DB, skipping auth routes'\)
" 2>&1)

from app import app, db, User

with app.test_client\(\) as c:
    with app.app_context\(\):
        user = User.query.first\(\)
        if user:
            # Temporarily mark onboarding incomplete
            original = user.onboarding_complete
            user.onboarding_complete = False
            db.session.commit\(\)
            
            with c.session_transaction\(\) as sess:
                sess['user_id'] = user.id
            
            r = c.get\('/onboarding/step1'\)
            has_disabled = b'nav-disabled' in r.data
            has_tooltip = b'Complete setup to access this section' in r.data
            print\(f'nav-disabled class present: {has_disabled}'\)
            print\(f'tooltip present: {has_tooltip}'\)
            
            # Restore
            user.onboarding_complete = original
            db.session.commit\(\)
" 2>&1)

            # Symptom forms

from app import app, db, User

with app.app_context\(\):
    with app.test_client\(\) as c:
        r = c.get\('/register'\)
        assert r.status_code == 200
        print\('register OK'\)

        user = User.query.first\(\)
        if user:
            with c.session_transaction\(\) as sess:
                sess['user_id'] = user.id
            r = c.get\('/settings'\)
            assert r.status_code == 200
            assert b'pw-strength' in r.data
            assert b'pw-match-error' in r.data
            assert b'pw-submit-btn' in r.data
            assert b'disabled' in r.data
            print\('settings OK — strength + match + disabled btn present'\)

            r = c.get\('/symptoms/new'\)
            assert r.status_code == 200
            assert b'over-limit' in r.data  # from the JS in base.html
            print\('new_symptom OK — over-limit logic present'\)

Bash(source .venv/bin/activate && python -c "
from app import app, db, User

with app.app_context\(\):
    with app.test_client\(\) as c:
        user = User.query.first\(\)
        with c.session_transaction\(\) as sess:
            sess['user_id'] = user.id

        # Check symptom forms have data-charlimit, NOT maxlength
        r = c.get\('/symptoms/new'\)
        html = r.data.decode\(\)
        assert 'data-charlimit=\\"200\\"' in html, 'missing data-charlimit=200'
        assert 'data-charlimit=\\"500\\"' in html, 'missing data-charlimit=500'
        assert 'maxlength' not in html, 'stale maxlength still present'
        assert 'name_count' in html
        assert 'description_count' in html
        print\('new_symptom OK'\)

        # Check edit symptom — submit button outside form
        symptoms = db.session.execute\(db.text\('SELECT id FROM symptoms WHERE user_id=:u LIMIT 1'\), {'u': user.id}\).fetchone\(\)
        if symptoms:
            r = c.get\(f'/symptoms/{symptoms[0]}/edit'\)
            html = r.data.decode\(\)
            assert 'data-charlimit=\\"200\\"' in html
            assert 'form=\\"symptomForm\\"' in html  # submit is outside
            print\('edit_symptom OK'\)

        # Check all other forms
        for path, name in [\('/protocols/new','protocol'\), \('/rescue-options/new','rescue'\), 
                           \('/episodes/new','episode'\), \('/experiments/new','experiment'\)]:
            r = c.get\(path\)
            html = r.data.decode\(\)
            assert 'data-charlimit' in html, f'{name}: missing data-charlimit'
            assert 'maxlength' not in html, f'{name}: stale maxlength'
            print\(f'new_{name} OK'\)

        print\('All forms verified'\)
" 2>&1)
